summary.prompt: Perform a deep dive into GTO poker theory and create a training application with multiple modes one of which highlights a curated selection of hands to walk through that identify edge cases of GTO play for training. Another mode should be a fully functional 9 person table that tracks stats over sessions and features a sampling of 'classic' poker player archetypes.
summary.raw_verdict: pass (PNV.1 verified end-to-end under jsdom production fidelity).

ledger.restatement: A Texas Hold'em GTO training application for a semi-professional player who struggles to practice pure GTO play. Contains at least two modes: (1) a guided walkthrough of a curated hand library showcasing GTO edge cases, and (2) a fully functional 9-handed table populated with classic poker player archetypes that records per-session statistics across sessions.

td.prompt_verb_chosen: play (a full session of 9-handed cash vs archetype agents with stats persisted across sessions)

timeline.notes: All IPs flagged at high or medium importance — IP1 (delivery surface) and IP2 (data source) are the cascade-loaded ones
All 5 IPs resolved via quick-reasoning. Prompt-named verb chosen as "play" (the 9-handed table session w/ persistence chain)
The Sev 0 (dev-002, quoted `'raise_3.5'` key) was caught by Integrator's `node --check` pass — exactly the false-alarm-prevention loop v1.1 specified
jsdom workaround for `file://` localStorage documented in CV report
Run-report (this file) is the last item

what_worked: Inline mode for 7 sections held up. — Coordinator-as-Overseer-as-Builder produced 56 files in a single dispatch. The substrate is faithful — section state files, dispatch log, deviations, history log, build-complete signal all present.

The PNV chain caught what per-section assertions wouldn't. — PNV.1 specified play → reload → play, which exercised cross-section state (table → stats writer → storage → reload → reconstitution). No single section's acceptance phrase tested…

Production-fidelity gate had teeth. — CV correctly identified that jsdom's `file://` localStorage rejection is an environment quirk, not a runtime-dep substitution, and documented the workaround. The artifact's runtime code path is unchang…

TD's quick-reasoning rubric saved a Researcher dispatch on every IP. — All five IPs were canonical-answer (browser vs desktop, embedded vs solver, quiz vs replay, static vs adaptive, dashboard vs full-history) — none warranted research giv…

GTO content depth. — The 22-hand library, 16-concept glossary, and 8-archetype roster with `contrast_with_gto` commentary live in the artifact (per TD's design choice to put the "deep dive" in the deliverable, not in a side document). This…

TaskCreate per-IP task creation by Discovery did not happen. — Discovery's v1.8 charter says "For each inflection point identified, TaskCreate a task." The Discovery agent didn't. The phase-backbone tasks were created by Orchestrator (me),…

TD's Researcher TaskCreate similarly didn't fire — , but for a benign reason: TD chose quick-reasoning for all five IPs, so no Researchers were dispatched and no Research tasks needed creating. v1.8 didn't anticipate the all-quick-reasonin…

PNV's "play 10 complete hands" is a heavy scenario for jsdom. — The CV agent had to drive ten full hands of 9-handed poker through an event loop without a real user — this required programmatic forcing of the hero's `decide` step. CV did i…
